seniority.level: Mid-level IC (IC-02, consistent with 3 years experience and $151K-$220K base)
level_signals: Minimum requirements call for 3 years of experience across multiple PM dimensions
Base salary range $151,070 - $219,600 consistent with mid-level IC at Stripe

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Bachelor's degree or foreign equivalent in engineering or a related field, followed by 3 years of experience in product design or engineering.
Bachelor's degree or foreign equivalent in engineering or a related field, followed by 3 years of experience in product design or engineering.
3 years of experience driving end-to-end product requirements across multiple teams;

3 years of experience in gathering and prioritizing complex deliverables while engaging with customers, managing technical and business trade-offs, and collaborating in a cross-functional environment with customers, business leaders, and technical teams to deliver complex data and platform products in the fintech industry;
3 years of experience working on user interface (UI) and user experience (UX) principles and best practices;
3 years of experience with software product management;
3 years of experience Utilizing data to manage metric, drive process improvement, and implement business cases;
3 years of experience in developing company-wide product roadmaps, generating feature requirements, and executing go-to-market strategies;
2 years of experience building Gen AI applications for fintech industry.
Drive the development of internal GenAI applications, including use case scoping, vendor evaluation, data readiness, and change management for operational adoption;
Conduct technical and business feasibility analysis for data center localization to support Stripe's global expansion, addressing compliance, latency, and operational readiness;
Lead efforts to platformize reporting by building reusable data models and internal tools that scale across multiple product and finance use cases;
Oversee the development of a semantic layer to standardize key metrics and accelerate self-service analytics for data consumers;
Collaborate closely with engineering, data science, legal, and compliance teams to ensure scalable, secure, and efficient product delivery.

green_flags: Up to 50% telecommuting permitted — meaningful flexibility
Multiple positions available — higher likelihood of hiring
Clear and specific responsibilities tied to real infrastructure challenges
Equity, 401(k), medical/dental/vision, and wellness stipends mentioned as additional benefits
red_flags: No cities listed — it is unclear where the role is based or what offices are involved
Work authorization and visa sponsorship not mentioned — unclear for international candidates
#LI-DNI tag indicates this role should not be indexed by LinkedIn — may limit candidate reach or indicate posting is compliance-driven (e.g. PERM labor certification)
3-year hard requirements stacked across many dimensions simultaneously may be a PERM/immigration compliance posting rather than a standard open role
UI/UX 3-year requirement is unusual for a data infrastructure and AI PM role — may be a PERM artifact

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from IC-02 title, 3-year experience floors, and comp range)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated in JD)
ambiguities: The #LI-DNI tag and rigidly stacked minimum requirements at exactly 3 years across all dimensions strongly suggest this may be a PERM labor certification posting rather than a standard open requisition. Candidates should verify with Stripe whether this is an active open role.
Location is not specified — '50% telecommuting permitted' implies physical presence required at some office, but which city or office is not stated.

Salary range description says 'this salary range represents the base salary range for the role and any sales commissions/sales bonuses targets, if applicable' — slightly ambiguous whether any portion includes variable comp or is purely base.
